meta.description: Soul traits are the permanent, hidden psychological core of every person in the game. They are never shown to the player as numbers or labels — the player discovers them only through observed behaviour over time. Each trait exists as one half of a pair; a person holds one side of each pair at generation. Reveal difficulty controls how many qualifying events the moment system requires before a reveal can trigger. These traits do not change — they are who a person is, not how they feel this week.

traits.description: Steps toward danger rather than away. Does not freeze under pressure or in unfamiliar situations.
Pulls back when the stakes rise. Will find reasons to avoid confrontation, risk, or exposure.
Stays level in chaos. Crisis does not distort their thinking or their body.
Pressure scrambles them. Small crises become large ones when they are involved.
Sees themselves accurately. Takes correction without ego. Credits others freely.
Overestimates themselves, underestimates others. Resists feedback. Needs to be the story.
Willing to wait for the right moment. Does not force outcomes. Trusts process over shortcuts.
Needs to see results now. Cuts corners when progress feels slow. Frustrates coaches and sparring partners.
Extends good faith. Believes people are working with them, not against them, until shown otherwise.
Reads threat into neutral events. Assumes ulterior motives. Hard to build a team around.
Keeps to the plan. Shows up, does the work, respects the structure. Reliable under accumulation.
Acts before thinking. Takes shortcuts that cost them later. Exciting to watch, expensive to manage.
Keeps going after setbacks. Treats failure as information. Does not quit in the dark.
Loses shape when things go wrong. A bad loss, a harsh word, or a cold streak can break their momentum for weeks.
Always wants more. Never fully satisfied with where they are. Drives themselves past comfort.
Settles at good enough. Does not burn for more. Hard to push past a comfortable plateau.
